_comment_2: Copy the relevant blocks into your agent's mcp_config.json. Edit manually — never let the agent edit this file.

StitchMCP._comment: REQUIRED — reads AI designs from Google Stitch

wp-elementor-mcp._comment: REQUIRED — WordPress page management via REST API

env.WORDPRESS_APPLICATION_PASSWORD: xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx

env.WP_APP_PASSWORD: xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx

_notes.credentials: Use the SAME username and Application Password in all 3 servers AND your Node.js scripts. Mismatched credentials cause HTTP 401 errors.
_notes.app_password_format: WordPress Application Passwords use spaces: 'xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx'. Create at: WP Admin → Users → Profile → Application Passwords.
_notes.api_key: Get Google API key at: https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials. Enable the Stitch API.
_notes.global_install: Install MCP servers globally: npm install -g wp-elementor-mcp elementor-mcp. Using npx causes startup timeouts.
_notes.never_edit_from_agent: NEVER let the agent edit mcp_config.json — it will freeze the IDE.
_notes.disable_unused_mcps: Set 'disabled: true' on all MCPs not needed for this project (Puppeteer, Figma, etc.) to prevent context dispersion.
_notes.scripts_credentials: Scripts (fix_slugs.js, replace_stitch_images.js) read credentials from env vars WP_URL, WP_USER, WP_APP_PASSWORD. Never hardcode them.
